<traits>
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 35

**Required Number of Abilities** 5

</column>

A pipefox is a tiny magical fox with a thin, limbless body that is about a foot long. Pipefoxes are secretive, shy, evasive, and they love to acquire knowledge, using innate divination magic to learn facts and languages over time. They can bond with a person sharing their scholarly pursuits after a long period of observation and careful social interactions.

<traits>
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=222" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 40

**Required Number of Abilities** 8

**Granted Abilities** [Flier](/Familiars.aspx?ID=7), [Lifelink](/Familiars.aspx?ID=16), [Skilled (Society)
](/Familiars.aspx?ID=33), [Speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=12), [Spellcasting](/Familiars.aspx?ID=34)

</column>

[Polongs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1891) are wicked, invisible spirits created from the blood of murder victims, bound to glass bottles, and fed with the blood of their master. They're often used to possess and sicken their master's enemies. Polongs can't be found or acquired, only created, a process that can take up to two weeks. If a polong's bottle is ever destroyed, or if its master fails to provide its daily feeding of blood, the polong withers and dies.

</column>

The ways of wizards are strange and mysterious, and nowhere is this more obvious than in the arcane predilection for animating random pieces of candy and using them as familiars. The Winter Witches of Irrisen are known for weaving sparrows or nightingales from gingerbread, while in Arcadia, sorcerers mold mice from spiced chocolate. Most famous, however, are the hard candy beetles of Nex, though a few radical scholars claim that they are in fact a demi-natural species, a product of the Mana Wastes' wild magic. Most mages dismiss such wild claims, however.

<traits>

<trait label="Celestial" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=551" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Abilities** [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=142), [Independent](/Familiars.aspx?ID=115), [Lifelink](/Familiars.aspx?ID=133), [Manual Dexterity](/Familiars.aspx?ID=146), [Speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=124)

</column>

The kinnari seats herself, pressing an instrument of gourds and strings to her shoulder. The kinnara stands ready, with his tail upright, preparing for his beloved’s rhythm. Kinnars are half-human, half-songbird lovers who sing, dance, and perform for and with each other, forever accompanying each other in their arts. Severance creates suffering, and neither can stay apart from the other else they both become heartbroken and die. While most kinnar pairings serving as familiars include a female kinnari and male kinnara, kinnar pairings of different genders aren’t uncommon. Kinnar familiars have access to all of their familiar abilities regardless of the genders of the kinnars.

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=599" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 45

**Required Number of Abilities** 4

**Granted Abilities** [Flier](/Familiars.aspx?ID=145), [Independent](/Familiars.aspx?ID=115), [Speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=124)

</column>

Mockingfey are tiny fey with the heads of humanoids and the bodies of colorful parrots. Though they’re mischievous and unruly, mockingfeys are very loyal to those they consider friends. They can speak and understand language, but typically communicate in pantomime instead, as they find verbal speech restrictive.

 Some spellcasters ally with mockingfey as familiars, as they’re highly curious and keen—if distractable— students. Cobyslarni students and faculty, being used to the bizarre nature of the First World, are more tolerant than most of a mockingfey’s antics, and the little creatures are a common sight flitting around the school’s towers. Naturally, any pranks a mockingfey pulls on a student’s rivals are merely the unsanctioned work of an excitable familiar.

 Cobyslarni representatives have found their familiars’ antics are less appreciated by the students of other schools. In part, this is because a small or feathered student doesn’t seem out of place among the Traveling Academy’s student body. Even without that confusion, many Cobyslarni students are finding they must teach their mockingfey familiars the most difficult lessons of all: when not to insult people and who to leave alone.

<traits>
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Foolish Housekeeping and Other Articles](/Sources.aspx?ID=239)

**Required Number of Abilities** 4

**Granted Abilities** [Construct](/Familiars.aspx?ID=109), [Independent](/Familiars.aspx?ID=115), [Play Dead](/Familiars.aspx?ID=167), [skilled (arcana)](/Familiars.aspx?ID=123)

</column>

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

If you, despite the warning, desire a dust bunny companion, or perhaps you have one by accident and are willing to care for it, this section is for you. Dust bunnies greatly enjoy company and will often desire to lie on your lap or desk. Wherever they keep you company, ensure the area is regularly cleaned of any new or shed debris; otherwise, another dust bunny may form.

  To feed your dust bunny, simply keep the collections from your daily housekeeping. However, if you notice they begin growing to an unreasonable size, be sure to limit their portions, as they have been known to grow to the size of large dogs.

  It's important to remember that having formed of their own accord, dust bunnies are highly independent. While this makes them great companions and assistants, this also makes dust bunnies prone to making mischief, often spreading dirt out of spite or simply by wasting away. One should be vigilant for such things, for more dust and debris creates the perfect environment for new dust bunnies to form, and one can quickly be overrun with an infestation of dust bunnies!

<title level="1" right="Specific Familiar " pfs="Standard">[Grindle-Drake](/Familiars.aspx?ID=55&Specific=true)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 100

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

**Access**
You’re from the Five Kings Mountains.

**Required Number of Abilities** 4

**Granted Abilities** [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=142), [skilled (Perception, Survival)](/Familiars.aspx?ID=123), [Touch Telepathy](/Familiars.aspx?ID=127)

</column>

Grindlegrubs have been an essential, if unglamorous, part of the Five Kings Mountains’ food system for millennia, playing an especially important role in populous cities where enormous pits of them break down all organic waste. In these pits, the large white grubs eat voraciously, breed quickly, and provide a ready source of protein up and down the food chain. The grindlegrubs within the Five Kings Mountains were specially bred millennia ago to enhance their size and other favorable traits. This domesticated breed of grindlegrub evolved to grow to enormous sizes and eventually metamorphosize into nigh-useless large black flies too heavy to fly. Knowledge of the origins of grindlegrubs and their original forms was lost to time as the creatures’ usefulness in their modified state proved invaluable to all settlements in the region. 

Centuries ago, Ylivsach Taarghan, a young scholar at the Tar-Kazmukh arcane library undertook a deep study of grindlegrubs, encouraging their metamorphosis under conditions both magical and mundane in an attempt to recreate the grindlegrubs of old. With the right combination of arcane energy and a sulfurous steam vent for incubation, Ylivsach succeeded in bringing back the scaly, foot-long creatures with bright orange and red hues as well as six legs, pointy tails, and sharp beaks. Naming these creatures grindle-drakes, Ylivsach provoked controversy with her discovery, from those who insist that these creatures aren’t related to drakes to those who insist that they aren’t metamorphosed grindlegrubs. To this day, grindle-drakes are bred in Tar-Kazmukh and sometimes gifted to friends of the Blue Warders.

<title level="1" right="Specific Familiar " pfs="Standard">[Golden Ermine](/Familiars.aspx?ID=57&Specific=true)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Access**
You're from Hermea

**Required Number of Abilities** 4

**Granted Abilities** [Climber](/Familiars.aspx?ID=141), [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=142), [Touch Telepathy](/Familiars.aspx?ID=127), [Valet](/Familiars.aspx?ID=128)

</column>

The golden ermine is a small mammal with a slender, elongated body. Their white fur is soft and dense, though their golden tails are what make them stand out the most from other stoats. The ermine’s legs are short, and it has sharp claws. Its head is small, with dark eyes and a pointed nose.

Stoats inhabit many different regions of Golarion, with their fur colors and markings differing based on location. These colors can range from pure white to dark brown and every color in between. The colors that they are most prized for are the hues on their tails, which appear as silver, bronze, or even gold. Unfortunately, their fur was so highly prized for making clothing that hunters began to kill them until they were almost extinct. Today, those with golden tails remain only in Hermea, where they are protected to ensure their survival.

Golden ermines are regarded as symbols of good fortune. When a citizen of Promise seeks a blessing of luck to overcome a significant challenge, they visit Rortinos Forest or the Altinar Mountains, where the stoats are known to establish their dens, bringing an offering of fresh rabbit. If an ermine comes out to eat the offering, it is considered the greatest honor, bestowing the good fortune that is being sought.

<title level="1" right="Specific Familiar " pfs="Standard">[Royal Gull](/Familiars.aspx?ID=58&Specific=true)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 51

**Access**
You're from Hermea

**Required Number of Abilities** 2

**Granted Abilities** [Flier](/Familiars.aspx?ID=145), [Speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=124)

</column>

The royal gull is a medium-sized bird with multicolored plumage and a golden beak. When it flaps its wings, its feathers release fine iridescent dust, leaving a shiny trail wherever it flies.

Adult royal gulls inhabit the rocky coastal areas of Hermea where they can easily find a wide variety of fish, which are their primary food source. However, royal gull chicks are unable to eat fish, and for their first year feed only on numchryes, a shrub-like fruit found only in the Rortinos Forest. This unique diet contributes to the vibrant colors of their plumage. During mating season, males fly in flocks over Promise, creating the illusion of moving rainbows as their fluttering wings release iridescent dust over the city. During this time, tailors and other artisans work quickly to collect this iridescent dust, as they can infuse it within dyes to create unique lustrous effects that can be used throughout the year.

text: Royal Gull Source High Seas pg. 51 Access You're from Hermea Required Number of Abilities 2 Granted Abilities Flier, Speech The royal gull is a medium-sized bird with multicolored plumage and a golden beak. When it flaps its wings, its feathers release fine iridescent dust, leaving a shiny trail wherever it flies. Adult royal gulls inhabit the rocky coastal areas of Hermea where they can easily find a wide variety of fish, which are their primary food source. However, royal gull chicks are unable to eat fish, and for their first year feed only on numchryes, a shrub-like fruit found only in the Rortinos Forest. This unique diet contributes to the vibrant colors of their plumage. During mating season, males fly in flocks over Promise, creating the illusion of moving rainbows as their fluttering wings release iridescent dust over the city. During this time, tailors and other artisans work quickly to collect this iridescent dust, as they can infuse it within dyes to create unique lustrous effects that can be used throughout the year.

<title level="1" right="Specific Familiar " pfs="Standard">[Wildtwig](/Familiars.aspx?ID=59&Specific=true)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 51

**Access**
You're from Hermea

**Required Number of Abilities** 6

**Granted Abilities** [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=142), [Plant](/Familiars.aspx?ID=120), [Plant Form](/Familiars.aspx?ID=121), [resistance (poison)](/Familiars.aspx?ID=122), [Speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=124), [Tough](/Familiars.aspx?ID=148)

</column>

This canine is unique, as its fur is made of soft leaves, rather than hair. Wildtwigs also have two wooden horns protruding from their head, which curve as they grow with age, similar to those of a ram. The color of their body changes with the seasons; in spring and summer they are vibrant shades of green, with vibrant flowers spotting their fur, whereas in the autumn and winter their fur changes to more muted shades of browns, reds, and oranges. No matter the season, a single, yellow-spotted, red peach grows from one of their horns, fruit that is believed to provide healing powers to those who eat it. 

Wildtwigs are only found within on Hermea. In fact, their existence was unknown until Mengkare visited Two Tree Island. Wildtwigs spend their lives near trees, helping them grow as their shedding leaves provide them with essential nutrients during the seasons when they need them most. Today, it is common to find wildtwigs in the shade of the island’s two main trees, changing their body tones as they do so. Additionally, on days Mengkare was on the island, a pair of wildtwigs could always be found at his side.

<document level="2" id="familiar-ability-193" />

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 229

**Access**
you're a [shadowcaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=101)

**Granted Abilities** [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=5), [Manual Dexterity](/Familiars.aspx?ID=10), [Master's Form](/Familiars.aspx?ID=28), [resistance (cold and negative)](/Familiars.aspx?ID=32), [Shadow Step](/Familiars.aspx?ID=52)

</column>

Some spellcasters and ritualists don't just summon and bind a familiar, but instead transform their own shadow into a familiar. These casters can be identified by their lack of a shadow regardless of the level of light in the area and by the strange muted appearance of any article of clothing or jewelry they wear. Occasionally mistaken for [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) or other [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) by the cautious or superstitious, these spellcasters nonetheless find the trade worth it in exchange for the unique abilities their shadow familiar can possess.

 Shadow familiars are a type of specific familiar. While all shadowcasters have access to these familiars, there are other paths to learn the proper magic to obtain a shadow familiar.

<title level="1" right="Specific Familiar " pfs="Standard">[Clockwork Familiar](/Familiars.aspx?ID=9&Specific=true)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 23

**Required Number of Abilities** 3

**Granted Abilities** [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

A clockwork creature is typically composed entirely of turning gears and small bronze steam pipes. It can take the form of any Tiny animal, such as a small mammal, reptile, or bird. Its eyes are cut from glittering glass, with many facets within to help it process what it sees. Any teeth, beak, and tongue are rendered out of iron. When it moves, its joints emit little croaks and whistles, and it occasionally emits electrical sparks if perturbed. Clockwork creatures who live near the sea, or are otherwise exposed to water often, might squeeze lemons and use the juice to wash themselves. They can also blow steam out of the pipes on their bodies to intimidate an enemy or put up a smokescreen. Your clockwork familiar is a specific familiar. Specific familiars (Advanced Player’s Guide 147) are familiars with unique abilities. In order for you to choose a specific familiar, your familiar must be capable of having the template’s required number of familiar abilities. A specific familiar gains the listed traits and several abilities, including abilities it can gain only as a specific familiar. Much like a familiar that naturally has a familiar ability, you can never swap out any of these granted or unique abilities. Once you’ve selected a specific familiar, you can’t change it without losing your familiar—this uses the same rules as if your familiar had died. If your familiar gains more abilities than necessary to become a specific familiar, you can spend the remaining abilities normally. If your clockwork familiar is destroyed, it can be brought back to life through any conventional means that would bring back a living familiar, such as through resurrection magic.
